# UC-12.5.11 · GitLab Secret Detection Scanner Block Events on Default Branch

## Description

Hard blocks on default branch indicate exposed credentials that must rotate immediately.

## Value

Prioritizes secret leaks on trunk so incident response starts before lateral movement.

## Implementation

Map scanner job names; include `commit_sha` and MR IID when present. Open Sev-1 if default branch blocks spike.

## SPL

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="gitlab:pipeline" OR sourcetype="gitlab:secret_detection"
| search (scanner="secret_detection" OR "Secret Detection" OR secret_detection) AND (blocked=1 OR status="failed")
| eval ref_norm=lower(ref)
| where ref_norm IN ("main","master","default","production")
| stats latest(_time) as last_block, values(ref) as branches by project_path, commit_sha
| sort -last_block
```

## Visualization

Table (project, commit, branch), Timeline of blocks.

## References

- [GitLab Add-on for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6848)
- [GitLab CI/CD logs to Splunk (Lantern)](https://lantern.splunk.com/Data_Descriptors/GitLab/Getting_Gitlab_CICD_data_out_of_a_Gitlab_Pipeline_into_Splunk)
